texto: I invited him over and he stayed forever. I feed him all my bread, the old peppers turned to mush & fluorescent mold, the chicken thighs unthawed & boneless. He ask for more so I give him my left leg. He ask for more & I give him my lungs & lips. More still, and there goes my bladder. He eats it all, all of me, eats the whole damn house, & I live forever in him or maybe it's the other way around. I swallowed. I swallowed him whole & didn't even notice him thrashing inside me.
explicacao: Citação de Danez Smith sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “I invited him over and he stayed forever. I feed him all my bread, the old peppers turned to mush…” articula vida, feed, forever em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como forever e invitation.

frase_original: I invited him over and he stayed forever. I feed him all my bread, the old peppers turned to mush & fluorescent mold, the chicken thighs unthawed & boneless. He ask for more so I give him my left leg. He ask for more & I give him my lungs & lips. More still, and there goes my bladder. He eats it all, all of me, eats the whole damn house, & I live forever in him or maybe it's the other way around. I swallowed. I swallowed him whole & didn't even notice him thrashing inside me.

texto: The dog upstairs won't shut up, but I can't hate him, he's up there alone all day, making noise must be the only way he knows he's not a ghost.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Danez Smith sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “The dog upstairs won't shut up, but I can't hate him, he's up there alone all day, making noise m…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, loneliness, upstairs, útil em momentos de reflexao.
